source-discipline.reason: v10.16 queue entry added with cited grounding: DR-2026-05-26 (this session's Pass 2a authored artifact), v10.15-rejected rationale (no PI bump unless critical armature) referenced by section anchor, and architecture v2.3 scope_assumptions (skill-assignment-only changes not requiring out-of-band PI bumps). v10.15-rejected section content is preserved unchanged.

canonical-workplan.reason: v10.16 entry adds a skills_assigned line for a new skill governed by an adopted-in-progress DR (DR-2026-05-26 PROPOSED, adopts upon pilot completion). The entry does not propose a standing-rule change; skill assignments are documentation, not gate.

adherence-logging-and-attestation.reason: This attestation IS the rule firing for the PI-update-needed.md edit. Commit carries [DOCTRINE: 61c7f95] before timestamp per v10.14 canonical form.

bias_direction: The v10.16 entry is named explicitly so the queue inventory does not silently grow. The bias risk is treating documentation-style PI changes as deserving the same level of bump-ceremony as standing-rule changes; the v10.15-rejected precedent argues both directions, and this entry sides with 'queue it; ship with next legitimate bump' rather than 'ship a v10.16 today.' The interpretation is owner-served by the rejected precedent; an alternative reading is that even skill-assignment changes warrant an owner paste so the live PI reflects current invocation vocabulary.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: An outside reviewer would observe that DRs proliferate across consecutive sessions (DR-2026-05-23, DR-2026-05-24, DR-2026-05-26), each adding queue entries that may not all ship under the same PI bump. The risk is queue drift - a stack of v10.16/v10.17/v10.18 entries each marked 'queued' but never actually deployed. Counter: each queue entry is structurally minimal (single skill line for v10.16); the v10.14 architecture-supplied bootstrap-decoupling removed the historical PI-bump pressure for mechanics changes; the queue can defensibly hold N entries until owner pastes them in a batch. Mitigation: this attestation explicitly cites the bundling rationale so the owner sees the intended deployment cadence.
